auth_notes: Most Lab Tracking systems (like LabTracking.com or custom LIMS) require an API Key passed in the header (e.g., X-API-KEY) or a Bearer token obtained via an OAuth2 client credentials flow.
rate_limits: Varies by provider; typically 60-100 requests per minute for standard clinical tiers.
gotchas: Lack of native webhook support requires building a polling mechanism using the n8n Schedule trigger.
Data often follows strict clinical standards (HL7 or FHIR) which may require complex JSON mapping or XML conversion.
Strict HIPAA compliance requirements may necessitate self-hosting n8n or using a HIPAA-compliant cloud instance.
client_must_provide: API Key or Client Credentials (ID and Secret)
Sample JSON/XML payload for a test lab order
research_notes: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Since native webhooks are rarely available in specialized lab software, the workflow must implement a 'polling' pattern: a scheduled trigger that fetches 'Recent Updates' and filters for new status changes. Data transformation will likely require the 'Code' node to handle clinical data formatting.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. Since native webhooks are rarely available in specialized lab software, the workflow must implement a 'polling' pattern: a scheduled trigger that fetches 'Recent Updates' and filters for new status changes.
